# Engineering Specification: RDF-to-LPG Converter
## Automated Structural Replication and Vector Synchronization Pipeline
### Document Reference: ES-R2L-CONV-2026-V1
### Status: Release / Production-Ready
### Authorship: Generated by NotebookLM (Ontology & Graph Architect)

---

## 1. Executive Summary

This specification outlines the technical design for the **RDF-to-LPG (Labeled Property Graph) Converter**. This specialized utility is designed to replicate the static terminological definitions (TBox) from a formal semantic store (**Apache Fuseki**) to a high-performance graph database (**FalkorDB**) [55, 61, 546]. 

While Apache Fuseki governs the formal, standard-compliant description logic (OWL 2 DL) and standard-based SPARQL endpoints [118, 536], FalkorDB serves as the real-time, low-latency contextual pathfinding and vector-based GraphRAG engine [26, 51, 826]. This converter bridges the two engines, systematically flattening complex OWL axioms, taxonomies, and SKOS labeling dictionaries into a structured property graph [63, 118, 318]. The converted graph enables sub-millisecond topological path searches and semantic vector lookups [26, 51].

---

## 2. Structural Translation Model (RDF to LPG)

The converter maps W3C Semantic Web resource representations to LPG equivalents using a deterministic, schema-preserving model [55, 622, 662]:

```
+----------------------------------------+       +-----------------------------------------+
|        W3C Semantic Web (RDF)          | ====> |      Labeled Property Graph (LPG)       |
+----------------------------------------+       +-----------------------------------------+
| owl:Class / skos:Concept               | ====> | Node with Label `:Class`                |
| rdfs:subClassOf                        | ====> | Directed Edge `-[:SUBCLASS_OF]->`       |
| owl:ObjectProperty (Domain & Range)    | ====> | Directed Edge `-[:{PROPERTY_NAME}]->`   |
| skos:prefLabel / rdfs:label            | ====> | Node Property `name` (string)           |
| skos:definition / rdfs:comment         | ====> | Node Property `definition` (string)     |
| skos:altLabel                          | ====> | Node Property `alt_labels` (string[])   |
+----------------------------------------+       +-----------------------------------------+
```

### 2.1 Node Extraction & Properties (`:Class`)
For every class resource identified, a single node is merged with the primary label `:Class` [277]:
*   `iri` *(String, Unique Key)*: The fully qualified Internationalized Resource Identifier (e.g., `https://spec.edmcouncil.org/fibo/ontology/FBC/FunctionalEntities/FinancialServicesEntities/DepositoryInstitution`) [332].
*   `short_name` *(String)*: Prefix-abbreviated local name (e.g., `fibo-fbc-fct-fse:DepositoryInstitution`) [332].
*   `name` *(String)*: Human-readable display label derived from `rdfs:label` or `skos:prefLabel` [118, 318].
*   `definition` *(String)*: Business definition text mapped from `skos:definition` or `rdfs:comment` [118, 318, 703].
*   `alt_labels` *(String Array)*: Synonyms and alternative terms collected from `skos:altLabel` properties [118, 318].
*   `embedding` *(Float Array, Optional)*: High-dimensional vector embedding of the generated concept profile [51, 893].

### 2.2 Hierarchical Edges (`-[:SUBCLASS_OF]->`)
Subclass assertions (`rdfs:subClassOf`) are transformed into directed edges with type `SUBCLASS_OF` [235]. The edge points from the specific subclass to the general superclass, maintaining strict downward property inheritance in the graph:
$$\text{(SubClassNode)} \xrightarrow{\text{:SUBCLASS\_OF}} \text{(SuperClassNode)}$$

### 2.3 Object Property Edges (`-[:{PROPERTY_NAME}]->`)
Custom OWL Object Properties linking two classes are converted into direct semantic edges [235]. 
*   **Edge Type:** Evaluates to the uppercase Local Name of the object property (e.g., `fibo-fnd-rel-rel:provides` maps to the edge type `-[:PROVIDES]->`) [244].
*   **Properties:**
    *   `iri` *(String)*: Full property URI (e.g., `https://spec.edmcouncil.org/fibo/ontology/FND/Relations/Relations/provides`) [332].
    *   `name` *(String)*: Human-readable label (e.g., `"provides"`) [236].
    *   `definition` *(String)*: Explanatory note or comment [236].

---

## 3. Pull-Based Extraction Queries (SPARQL)

The converter extracts metadata incrementally by executing three coordinated SPARQL queries against Apache Fuseki's read-only SPARQL endpoint [283, 346, 536]:

### Query A: Core Classes & Lexical Labels Extraction
This query extracts all class-level entities, their canonical names, descriptions, and collapses multiple synonym assertions into a unified delimiter-separated string to ensure easy parsing:

```sparql
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX skos: <http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?class ?label ?definition (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?alt; separator="||") as ?alt_labels)
WHERE {
  ?class rdf:type owl:Class .
  FILTER(isIRI(?class))
  OPTIONAL { ?class rdfs:label ?label }
  OPTIONAL { ?class skos:definition ?definition }
  OPTIONAL { ?class skos:altLabel ?alt }
}
GROUP BY ?class ?label ?definition
```

### Query B: Subclass Taxonomy Extraction
This query extracts the hierarchical relationship tree of the TBox while filtering out system-level root entities (such as `owl:Thing`) [241, 417]:

```sparql
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>

SELECT ?subClass ?superClass WHERE {
  ?subClass rdfs:subClassOf ?superClass .
  FILTER(isIRI(?subClass) && isIRI(?superClass) && ?superClass != owl:Thing)
}
```

### Query C: Object Property Schema Linkage
This query extracts custom object properties, their domain (source), and range (target) restrictions to define valid traversal paths across nodes [235]:

```sparql
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?property ?label ?domain ?range ?definition WHERE {
  ?property rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty .
  ?property rdfs:domain ?domain .
  ?property rdfs:range ?range .
  OPTIONAL { ?property rdfs:label ?label }
  OPTIONAL { ?property rdfs:comment ?definition }
  FILTER(isIRI(?property) && isIRI(?domain) && isIRI(?range))
}
```

---

## 4. Labeled Property Graph Mutation (Cypher)

The converter compiles extracted RDF results into optimized, parameter-driven Cypher query batches for execution on FalkorDB:

### 4.1 Merging Class Nodes
Creates the class entity, maps its metadata, and formats alternate labels into a list [118, 318]:

```cypher
UNWIND $class_batch AS row
MERGE (c:Class {iri: row.class_iri})
SET c.short_name = row.short_name,
    c.name = COALESCE(row.name, row.short_name),
    c.definition = COALESCE(row.definition, "No definition available."),
    c.alt_labels = SPLIT(row.alt_labels, "||")
```

### 4.2 Merging Taxonomy Hierarchies
Establishes the directed taxonomic inheritance linkage between verified classes [235]:

```cypher
UNWIND $taxonomy_batch AS row
MATCH (sub:Class {iri: row.sub_iri})
MATCH (sup:Class {iri: row.super_iri})
MERGE (sub)-[:SUBCLASS_OF]->(sup)
```

### 4.3 Merging Semantic Object Relationships
Dynamically creates object property connections. Because Cypher does not support parameterized edge labels directly, relationship types are sanitized and injected dynamically in the application driver, while the metadata parameters are bound safely:

```cypher
// Relationship type (e.g., PROVIDES) is injected as an active token {rel_type}
UNWIND $relation_batch AS row
MATCH (dom:Class {iri: row.domain_iri})
MATCH (ran:Class {iri: row.range_iri})
MERGE (dom)-[r:{rel_type}]->(ran)
SET r.iri = row.prop_iri,
    r.name = row.prop_name,
    r.definition = row.prop_definition
```

---

## 5. Active GraphRAG and Semantic Vector Alignment

To enable the **Entity Linking (EL)** stage of the Ontology Assistant, we must allow language models to match natural language queries to our classes via vector search [51, 146, 550]:

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                Generated Concept Profile                                |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| "Class: MediumSizedCompany. Synonyms: middle-size company, medium enterprise.           |
|  Definition: a business entity whose size in assets or revenue falls within             |
|  a middle-tier classification. Superclasses: LegalEntity."                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
                                             │
                                             ▼
                        +-----------------------------------------+
                        | Local Embedding Model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) |
                        +-----------------------------------------+
                                             │
                                             ▼
                        +-----------------------------------------+
                        |  FalkorDB Vector Index Update (Cypher)  |
                        +-----------------------------------------+
```

### 5.1 Concept Profile Generation
For each processed class, the system pre-computes an expressive **Concept Profile Document** to supply rich contextual sentences for vector space encoding [550, 552]:

$$\text{Profile} = \text{"Class: } [\text{Label}] \text{. Synonyms: } [\text{AltLabels}] \text{. Definition: } [\text{Definition}] \text{. Superclasses: } [\text{Ancestors}] \text{."}$$

*Example Profile Output:*
> *"Class: MediumSizedCompany. Synonyms: middle-size company, medium enterprise. Definition: a business entity whose size in assets or revenue falls within a middle-tier classification. Superclass: LegalEntity."* [i]

### 5.2 Embedding Synthesis
The generated profile is processed through a fast, local sentence-transformer model (e.g., `all-MiniLM-L6-v2`) to yield a normalized 384-dimensional float vector:

```python
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer

model = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')
embedding_vector = model.encode(concept_profile_text).tolist()
```

### 5.3 Vector Index Update (Cypher)
The resulting float array is upserted back onto the FalkorDB node property, and registered with the schema search index to support sub-millisecond similarity queries [51]:

```cypher
MATCH (c:Class {iri: $class_iri})
SET c.embedding = $embedding_vector
```

---

## 6. Pipeline Sync & Invalidation Architecture

To maintain synchronization and prevent query path errors across the polyglot persistence layer, the pipeline implements an event-driven synchronization workflow [162, 170]:

```
[Ontology Modification] ──► [Incremental Sync Script] ──► [FalkorDB Upsert]
                                                                │
                                                                ▼
                                                       [Redis Cache Flush]
```

1.  **Sync Trigger:** A webhook is registered on the primary ontology editor (e.g., Protégé, TopBraid, or VocBench) [216, 310]. Any class addition, property range adjustment, or synonym edit triggers an HTTP POST payload to the converter.
2.  **Incremental Execution:** The converter queries Fuseki's delta logs for modified class IRIs, extracts their specific TBox fragments, re-runs vector generation, and updates only those nodes in FalkorDB [51].
3.  **Cache Invalidation:** Upon a successful graph update, the sync script issues an unconditional `clear()` command to the in-memory **Redis Schema Pruning Cache** [139, 140]. This ensures that stale, pre-computed paths are evicted immediately, forcing the next user request to compile across the updated ontology [140].
4.  **Transaction Logging:** Every replication event writes an audit record containing the modified triple count, execution timestamp, and source-to-target lineages to a persistent, read-only transaction log to meet strict financial data governance standards [140].

---
*End of Technical Specification.*
